reasoning: The Lean statement captures all components of the informal claim: p is surjective (h), continuous (hc), closed (hp : IsClosedMap p), and has compact fibers (∀ y, IsCompact (p ⁻¹' {y})), plus Y is compact (CompactSpace Y), with conclusion that X is compact (CompactSpace X). The definitions match: IsClosedMap means images of closed sets are closed, and CompactSpace matches "compact" in the topological sense. Although two hypotheses are both named `hp` (a naming shadow), both are still present as typed parameters that must be supplied, so the logical content is unchanged. The formalization is logically equivalent to the informal statement.
